taskDescription: Arrange the structures of the thoracic cage from superior to inferior.
paragraphs: Jugular (suprasternal) notch — the palpable dip at the top of the manubrium, between the two clavicles.
Sternal angle (Angle of Louis) — the ridge where the manubrium meets the body of the sternum, marking the level of the 2nd costal cartilage and T4/T5 disc.
Body of the sternum — the longest part, articulating with costal cartilages of ribs 2 through 7.
Xiphoid process — the small cartilaginous tip at the lower end of the sternum, a danger zone during CPR.
Costal margin — the lower border formed by the costal cartilages of ribs 7-10, marking the boundary between thorax and abdomen.
